summary: Completed frontend testing of OpenClaw Mission Control iteration 7 - Major feature additions. All 28 requested features tested and verified. Key changes: conversation threading with localStorage persistence, Settings page with 5 Claude-style tabs, Spaces with Files/Design/Development defaults, Agent creative workspace with capability toggles, live Jobs monitoring, Customize page with skill toggles.
passed_tests: Model selector: panels are flush side-by-side (no gap causing hover flashing)
Model selector: hover over provider shows models panel smoothly (left of providers)
Model selector: selecting a model updates label persistently (e.g., 'Claude Opus 4.6')
New thread button: clicking creates a new empty thread and clears messages
Thread history: sending a message auto-creates and saves a thread in sidebar 'Recents'
Thread history: clicking a thread in sidebar loads its messages
Thread history: X button deletes a thread (remaining threads: 0 after deletion)
Settings > General: Theme (Dark/Light/System), Language, Web Search toggle, Writing Style (Normal/Concise/Formal/Explanatory), Default Model
Settings > Profile: Name input, Email input, Custom Instructions textarea
Settings > Connected Apps: All 6 connectors shown with toggles (Control your Mac, Desktop Commander, File Access, Web Search, Signal, Telegram)
Settings > Connected Apps: ALL connectors OFF by default (verified after localStorage clear)
Settings > Connected Apps: MCP Servers and API Keys sections present with 'Add' buttons
Settings > Data Controls: Save history, Allow usage, Memory toggles present
Settings > Security: Two-factor auth toggle, Active sessions with Manage button
Spaces: Shows Files, Design, Development cards with colored icons (blue, pink, green)
Spaces: 'Add Space' dashed card present
Spaces: 'New Space' button works - creates new space with input field
Spaces: Clicking a space card opens detail view with threads (shows 'No conversations in this space yet')
Agent page: Shows 4 capability toggles (Image Generation, Design Creation, Code Execution, Web Browsing)
Agent page: Code Execution is ON by default (green border styling rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.25))
Agent page: Warning banner links to Settings when no connectors enabled ('Connect apps in Settings for full agent capabilities')
Agent page: Typing and clicking Build sends message with active capability chips (shows 'codeExec' chip)
Jobs page: Shows 'Active' section with running jobs (2 progress bars, 2 cancel buttons)
Jobs page: Shows 'History' section with completed/failed jobs
Jobs page: Active badge count shows in sidebar nav (shows '2', then '1' after cancel)
Jobs page: Cancel button works (job moved to History with 'cancelled' status)
Customize page: Shows toggleable skills list (all 8 skills: deep-research, code-review, web-scraper, file-manager, task-scheduler, mcp-builder, slack-gif-creator, canvas-design)
Customize page: 'Manage Connectors' links to Settings > Connected Apps
Chat: sending a message creates thread in sidebar, message appears with streaming response
Cowork: inline conversation works - click task opens conversation view with AI responses and progress sidebar
Code: terminal works (echo Hello World outputs 'Hello World')
seed_data_creation: None - using mock data from Zustand store
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 28+ requested features tested and working. Key iteration 7 changes verified: 1) Conversation threading with localStorage persistence (create, load, delete threads), 2) Settings page with 5 Claude-style tabs (General, Profile, Connected Apps, Data Controls, Security), 3) Connectors ALL OFF by default (verified after localStorage clear), 4) Spaces with Files/Design/Development defaults + custom space creation, 5) Agent page with 4 capability toggles (Code Execution ON by default), 6) Jobs page with Active/History sections and live badge count, 7) Customize page with skill toggles and link to Settings. All APIs remain MOCKED via Zustand store.
mocked_apis_note: ALL APIs are MOCKED via Zustand store - no real backend calls. Mocked: sendMessage (simulated chat response with streaming), executeCommand (simulated terminal output), switchModel (state update), cancelJob (state update), approveRequest/rejectRequest (state update), createThread/deleteThread (localStorage), addSpace (localStorage), Agent responses (simulated with setTimeout delay)
